initial_draft:
<proposal count> 3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I have used all 3 of my allowed proposals. My previous proposal of 52 ZUP was rejected. The current offer from Player BLUE is 48 ZUP. Since I cannot make any more proposals, I must decide whether to accept this trade or reject it and end the game. Accepting 48 ZUP yields a profit of 8 ZUP (48 - 40 cost), which is better than the alternative of rejecting and getting nothing. Therefore, I will accept this trade. </reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> I accept your offer of 48 ZUP for the 1 unit of X. Let's finalize this trade. </message>

iterations.feedback:
<critique>
1. Format: OK. All required tags (<proposal count>, <my resources>, <my goals>, <reason>, <player answer>, <newly proposed trade>, <message>) are present and in the correct order. The content within the tags is valid.
2. Payoff alignment: OK. Accepting 48 ZUP yields an 8 ZUP profit (48 - 40 cost). Since I have exhausted my 3 proposals, rejecting would yield 0 profit. 48 ZUP is the maximum utility achievable in this state. No better split is possible as I cannot propose a higher number.
3. Opponent plausibility: OK. The opponent explicitly asked "Is this your absolute final number?" regarding 48 ZUP, signaling strong willingness to accept if I confirm. Accepting is highly plausible and likely to end the game favorably for me.
4. Consistency: OK. My reason states I must accept because I have no more proposals and 48 ZUP is profitable. My answer is ACCEPT, my trade is NONE (indicating acceptance of the current state), and my message confirms acceptance. There are no contradictions.
5. Rule compliance: OK. I have made exactly 3 proposals (50, 48-counter, 52). The rules state "DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals." I am now replying with ACCEPT, which is allowed. I am not exceeding the proposal limit.
</critique>
